combine.title: Combine PDF files
combine.intro: Add two or more PDF files to merge them into a single document.
combine.reorderHint: Reorder with the arrows; the combined document follows this order.
combine.pickerFailed: Could not open the file picker: {{message}}
combine.empty: No files yet. Add at least two to combine.
combine.needTwo: Add at least two files to combine.
combine.perRowNote: Per-row page-range editing arrives in a later phase.

confirmClose.title: Unsaved changes
confirmClose.body: This document has unsaved changes. Save before closing?

exportEngine.title: Export to PDF
exportEngine.note: The hybrid engine selector picks pdf-lib by default and falls back to Chromium for encrypted or structurally-flagged PDFs. Phase 2 wires the actual export pipeline; this dialog ships now so the UX is consistent.

export.title: Export to Office
export.fmtWordDesc: Editable text document with paragraphs, headings, tables.
export.fmtExcelDesc: Workbook of tables detected via line-grid analysis; best for tabular PDFs.
export.fmtPptxDesc: One slide per page; positioned text and embedded images.
export.fmtJpegDesc: Lossy raster image; quality slider in next step.

ocrRun.title: Run OCR
ocrRun.invalidateTitle: Re-running OCR will replace existing results
ocrRun.invalidateBody: OCR results for this document already exist. Running again will replace them. Existing signatures may be invalidated if the text layer changes.
ocrRun.notPublicationQuality: OCR text is best-effort recognition, not publication-quality transcription. Always verify against the original.
ocrRun.honestyReminderAccuracy: OCR accuracy depends on scan quality; low-confidence words may be incorrect and will be highlighted in orange. OCR runs locally — no cloud upload; language packs are downloaded from Tesseract's official upstream on first use.
ocrRun.honestyReminderDuplicate: Re-running OCR adds another text layer. If this page already contains OCR text from a prior pass, multiple OCR passes may produce duplicate selectable text. Undo the previous pass first if you want clean output.
ocrRun.invalidateHeading: This PDF has a cryptographic signature
ocrRun.invalidateBody1: Running OCR modifies the document's page content, which will invalidate any prior PAdES signature. The signature audit log will record this invalidation; the signed file on disk is unchanged until you Save.
ocrRun.invalidateBody2: If you proceed, the original signature is no longer verifiable. You can re-sign the document after OCR.
ocrRun.dontAskAgain: Don't ask me again for this session
ocrRun.confirmCancel: Cancel OCR? Partial output will be discarded.
ocrRun.cancelledBody: The OCR run was cancelled. No changes were applied to the document.
ocrRun.summaryNotLoaded: OCR finished but the summary is not yet loaded.
ocrRun.doneReminderBody: OCR-extracted text becomes part of the document and is committed to disk on Save. Low-confidence words may be incorrect; review them before relying on the extracted text for legal or regulatory use.

padesSign.title: Sign with PFX certificate
padesSign.notLegalAdvice: A digital signature proves the file was not altered after signing; it is not legal advice and does not by itself make a document legally binding.
padesSign.passwordNeverSaved: Your password is never logged or saved. It's discarded immediately after the cert is loaded.
padesSign.passwordDiscarded: Your password is never logged or saved. It was discarded immediately after the certificate was loaded.
padesSign.errPfxDecode: PFX could not be decoded. Is the file valid?
padesSign.clickSignInfo: Click Sign to apply the signature. This may take up to 30 seconds if timestamping is enabled.

signatureCapture.title: Add visual signature
signatureCapture.drawnCanvasLabel: Drawing signature with mouse or pen — use the Image tab to upload instead
signatureCapture.dragDropImage: Drag-drop a PNG or JPEG into this area to import.
signatureCapture.errUnsupportedImage: Only PNG or JPEG images are supported.
signatureCapture.errImageDecode: The image could not be decoded.

languagePack.title: Language Packs
languagePack.offlineNote: Language packs are downloaded once and used offline thereafter.
languagePack.honestyReminder: Language packs download from Tesseract's official upstream (tessdata.projectnaptha.com). Every download is SHA-256-verified against the shipped catalog. OCR itself runs locally — no PDF bytes ever leave this machine.

scan.title: Scan from device
scan.deferred: Scanner integration arrives in Phase 5.1. For now, scan with your operating system's scan utility, then open or drag the saved PDF here to run OCR.
scan.deferredBody: Phase 5 ships the file-import + OCR path only. Native scanner (WIA/TWAIN) bindings are deferred to Phase 5.1 — no MIT-compatible Node WIA binding survives the project's open-source maturity bar at this time.
scan.saveThenDrag: Save the scan as PDF, then drag it into this app. You can then run OCR via Tools → Run OCR.

imageImport.title: Insert Image
imageImport.modeOverlay: As an overlay on the current page
imageImport.errUnsupported: Only PNG, JPEG, and TIFF files are supported.
imageImport.multiPageTiffWarning: Multi-page TIFF: only the first page will be used.
imageImport.userSpaceNote: Position is in PDF user-space (origin bottom-left). After insert, you can click-drag the overlay handles on the canvas to refine placement.

mailMerge.title: Mail Merge
mailMerge.flattenForms: Flatten forms in output (irreversible interactivity loss)

help.title: PDF Viewer & Editor — Help
help.intro: Welcome — pick a topic on the left, or jump straight to Keyboard shortcuts. Press Esc to close this dialog.
help.shortcutsLead: All shipped shortcuts. Many actions also have toolbar + menu equivalents.

gettingStarted.heading: Getting started
gettingStarted.openIntro: From the empty state you have two routes — both do the same thing:
gettingStarted.openBullet1: Open PDF… opens a native file picker (one file at a time).
gettingStarted.openBullet2: Drag a .pdf from File Explorer onto the window.
gettingStarted.openBullet3: File → Open Recent lists the last 20 files (persisted in SQLite).
gettingStarted.openLimits: Default max file size is 500 MB (configurable in Settings). The app validates the %PDF- magic header; encrypted PDFs that pdf.js cannot load surface a “Failed to open” toast — there is no password prompt yet.
gettingStarted.navIntro: The main viewer is centered; the left sidebar holds thumbnails and bookmarks; the right inspector is collapsed by default.
gettingStarted.navBullet1: Page Up / Page Down — previous / next page.
gettingStarted.navBullet3: Click the page-number field in the status bar to jump to a page.
gettingStarted.navBullet4: Click any thumbnail to jump to that page.
gettingStarted.zoomIntro: Zoom is fluid and cursor-anchored — the content point under your cursor stays under your cursor as you Ctrl+scroll.
gettingStarted.zoomBullet1: Ctrl+wheel — zoom in / out at the cursor (Acrobat-style anchoring).
gettingStarted.zoomBullet3: Ctrl+0 — reset to 100%. Ctrl+1 — Fit width. Ctrl+2 — Fit page.
gettingStarted.zoomBullet4: Click + drag on empty canvas area to pan.
gettingStarted.zoomFootnote: The current zoom is shown in the status bar. The zoom dropdown stays in sync with whatever Ctrl+scroll settled on.

editing.heading: Editing pages
editing.intro: Page edits live in memory, run through the undo stack, and are written into the saved bytes on Save. The edit-replay engine handles rotations, deletions, reorders, insert-blanks, image inserts, text replacements, and overlay placements.
editing.reorderBody: Drag a thumbnail to a new position. Hold Ctrl to multi-select, Shift to extend a range, Ctrl+A to select all.
editing.insertBody: Right-click any thumbnail for Insert blank before / Insert blank after / Delete page. Selected pages can be deleted with Delete or Backspace.
editing.rotateBody: Ctrl+R rotates clockwise; Ctrl+Shift+R counter-clockwise. Rotations persist on Save.
editing.combineBody: File → Combine PDFs… picks multiple PDFs and merges them into the open document at a chosen insertion point.
editing.imageBody: Insert → Image lets you place a PNG / JPEG / TIFF either as a new page or as an overlay on an existing page. Vector formats are not supported.
editing.textBody: Text editing is replace-only with the original font. Use the Text tool, click a text block, and type. If the font cannot encode a character, save fails with missing_glyph rather than silently replacing it — see Troubleshooting.
editing.undoBody: Ctrl+Z / Ctrl+Y. The replay engine restores deleted original pages byte-for-byte from the original document bytes.

annotations.heading: Annotations
annotations.intro: Phase 2 ships a full annotation toolkit. All annotations live in the document and are written into the saved bytes.
annotations.highlightBody: Press H, then drag across the text you want to mark. Color and opacity adjust in the Inspector.
annotations.stickyBody: Press S, click anywhere on a page to drop a note. Double-click to edit; the body persists.
annotations.textBoxBody: Press T, click-drag to place a text box. Inline text editing follows the same replace-only constraint as page text.
annotations.freehandBody: Freehand is pointer-only by design — no keyboard equivalent for an arbitrary stroke. The drawing surface has an accessible name describing the keyboard-operable alternatives (toolbar + Inspector).
annotations.shapeBody: Square, circle, polygon, arrow, callout, line-measure, polyline-measure. Measure tools support a calibration step — set a known distance, then subsequent measures convert to real units.
annotations.summaryBody: Open the Annotation summary inspector tab to see every annotation in the document grouped by page, with click-to-navigate.

forms.heading: Forms
forms.intro: Phase 3 ships AcroForm fill + a form designer + mail merge. XFA forms are read-only.
forms.fillBody: Open a PDF that contains form fields and they become editable immediately. Text fields, checkboxes, radios, dropdowns, and signature fields are all supported. The Forms sidebar tab shows a checklist of every field grouped by page.
forms.commitBody: Field edits commit to the document on blur (or explicit Save). The form-fill flow batches the whole fill into one undo step so Ctrl+Z reverts the entire fill, not field-by-field.
forms.designerBody: Tools → Toggle Form Designer mode. Drag a field type from the palette onto a page; resize and label it; rename via the Inspector. Save the design as a template (.fvet) for reuse.
forms.templateBody: Templates capture field positions + names + types (not values). Load a template into a fresh PDF to apply the same form structure.
forms.honestyBody: If the source PDF contains JavaScript actions, they are stripped on save and a toast tells you. XFA forms surface a banner indicating values cannot be filled in this engine.

mailMerge.heading: Mail merge
mailMerge.intro: Drive a form template from a CSV or Excel file to batch-produce filled PDFs.
mailMerge.step1: Load or design a form template (Forms tab).
mailMerge.step2: Tools → Mail merge → pick a CSV or .xlsx file.
mailMerge.step3: Map data columns to form field names (auto-match by name).
mailMerge.step4: Pick an output folder + filename template (supports column placeholders like {{LastName}}.pdf).
mailMerge.step5: Start. The runner shows per-row progress with a Cancel button and a Warnings tab.
mailMerge.limits: XLSX uses sheet 1 only (the banner says so). JavaScript form actions are stripped on each output file. The runner skips rows whose required fields are blank and surfaces them in Warnings.

signing.heading: Signing
signing.intro: Two independent signing flows: visual signatures (typed / drawn / image — quick paperwork sign-offs) and PAdES cryptographic signing (real digital signature with certificate + timestamp).
signing.visualBody: Tools → Sign opens the Signature Capture modal. Three tabs: Typed (pick a script-style font), Drawn (mouse / pen / touch), Image (upload PNG / JPEG). Place the captured signature on the page with the placement overlay.
signing.padesBody: Tools → Apply PAdES signature steps through Cert → Reason → Place. The cert + password live in memory only; nothing is logged or written to disk except the cryptographic envelope on the signed PDF.
signing.auditBody: The signature-audit inspector tab lists every signature applied to the open document with verifier output (valid / invalidated-by-subsequent-edit / TSA reachability).

ocr.heading: OCR
ocr.intro: Run Tesseract OCR over scanned or image-only PDFs to make them searchable. OCR runs locally — nothing leaves your machine.
ocr.runBody: Tools → OCR → pick page range + language + tier (fast / accurate). The job runs in the background; the OCR Results sidebar tab shows confidence per page.
ocr.langBody: English is bundled; nine other languages (Spanish, French, German, Portuguese, Italian, Russian, Simplified + Traditional Chinese, Japanese) download on demand from a trusted mirror with SHA-256 integrity verification.
ocr.confidenceBody: Toggle the confidence overlay to color-tint low-confidence words. Review low-confidence pages before trusting the OCR output.

scan.heading: Scan
scan.intro: On Windows, the app exposes connected WIA scanners (TWAIN devices that expose a WIA shim work too).
scan.discoveryBody: File → Scan from device lists detected scanners. macOS and Linux: this menu is disabled (scanner_unavailable).
scan.captureBody: Pick a device, choose DPI / color / size, scan. Pages append to the open document by default; flip the modal toggle to start a new document instead.
scan.ocrCombineBody: After scanning, run OCR on the new pages (Tools → OCR) to make the scanned text searchable + selectable.

export.heading: Export
export.intro: Export the open document (with edits + annotations + filled forms) to Word, Excel, PowerPoint, or image formats. The source PDF is never mutated.
export.formatsBullet4: PNG / JPEG / TIFF — page rasters at chosen DPI; multi-page TIFF bundles one file.
export.formatsBullet5: Searchable PDF — post-OCR, exports a copy with the OCR text layer baked in.
export.tiersBody: Layout-preserving (default for Word + PowerPoint) reconstructs columns, tables, and images. Text-only (default for Excel) is faster and produces flat text. Image formats use DPI instead of a tier.
export.queueBody: Once you click Start the modal closes; the job runs in the background; progress shows in the status-bar widget and the Exports sidebar tab. Cancel is always available.

shortcuts.heading: Keyboard shortcuts
shortcuts.lead: All currently-shipped shortcuts. Many actions also have toolbar + menu equivalents.

trustFloor.heading: Honesty banner
trustFloor.intro: Six obligations the app keeps regardless of feature surface. The full text + rationale lives in the User Guide; the short version follows.
trustFloor.telemetryBody: When enabled, only anonymous feature-usage counts are recorded — never document content, file paths, or personal information. Today nothing leaves your machine; the transport is an in-memory buffer you can inspect (Settings → View collected data).
trustFloor.updatesHeading: Auto-update is wired but the publish target is a placeholder
trustFloor.updatesBody: Update checks honestly report “update channel not configured” until a real release channel is published. The update client itself is ready; a code-signing certificate is also required to apply downloaded bundles.
trustFloor.ocrBody: Low-confidence words may be misread. Review before trusting OCR output for legal / financial use. OCR runs locally; nothing is uploaded.
trustFloor.signBody: Certificates + passwords live in memory only — never logged, never written to disk. A PAdES signature can be invalidated by subsequent edits; the audit panel tells you when this has happened.
trustFloor.exportBody: PDF → Office conversion preserves layout best-effort. Complex multi-column layouts, borderless tables, decorative typography, and XFA forms may not convert faithfully. Review the output before relying on it.
trustFloor.i18nBody: Roughly 68% of strings are translated to prove the framework; the rest fall back to English. Never a raw key — see Settings → General → Interface language.

troubleshooting.heading: Troubleshooting
troubleshooting.intro: Common issues and where to find diagnostics.
troubleshooting.openHeading: “Failed to open: not a PDF” / “Too large”
troubleshooting.openBody: The file failed the %PDF- magic-header check, or it exceeds the configured max file size (Settings → Files → Max file size).
troubleshooting.saveBody: An edit was clipped against page bounds, or a text edit used a character the original font can't encode. Adjust the edit; do not retry the same Save.
troubleshooting.saveGenericBody: Open the developer console (View → Toggle Developer Tools) for the full error. Send the message to the issue tracker.
troubleshooting.dndHeading: Drag-and-drop a PDF doesn't open it
troubleshooting.dndBody: Drag-and-drop relies on Electron's File.path. If a future Electron version disables it, use File → Open instead. Locked instruction L-001 guards this regression in CI.
troubleshooting.xfaBody: XFA is an older Adobe-proprietary form format. The app shows the static template only. Flatten the form (Forms designer → Flatten) before exporting to capture the values as text.
troubleshooting.jsActionsBody: By policy the engine strips JS actions on save. A toast tells you each time it happens. The visible form structure is preserved.
troubleshooting.ocrFailBody: Install the language pack from Settings → Languages, or pick a different OCR language for this job.
troubleshooting.signFailBody: Re-enter the certificate password (it is never cached), or use a valid cert. Expired certs cannot sign.
troubleshooting.logsBody: Main-process logs: %APPDATA%/PDF Viewer & Editor/logs/main.log. Renderer console: View → Toggle Developer Tools.

about.heading: About this app
about.intro: PDF_Viewer_Editor is an MIT-licensed desktop PDF viewer and editor built on permissive open-source libraries only — no AGPL, no commercial SDKs.
about.versionLine: See Help → About for the live version, runtime info, and the “Check for updates” button.
about.credits: Built with pdf.js (Apache-2.0), pdf-lib (MIT), tesseract.js (Apache-2.0), docx / exceljs / pptxgenjs (MIT), i18next / react-i18next / electron-updater (MIT), node-signpdf (MIT), better-sqlite3 (MIT), and more.

help.escToClose: Press Esc or click outside this dialog to close.

about.title: About PDF_Viewer_Editor
about.tagline: An original, permissively-licensed desktop PDF viewer and editor.
about.installConfirmBody: You have unsaved changes. Installing the update will restart the app. Save your changes first, or discard them and install now.
about.statusUpToDate: You are running the latest available version.
about.statusNotConfigured: Update channel not configured (placeholder). Updates check GitHub releases, but the release channel is a placeholder until the project is published — updates will not download until it is configured. The update client itself is wired and ready.
about.licenseBody: PDF_Viewer_Editor is open-source under the MIT license.
about.builtWith: Built with: pdf.js (Apache-2.0), pdf-lib (MIT), tesseract.js (Apache-2.0), docx / exceljs / pptxgenjs (MIT), i18next / react-i18next / electron-updater (MIT), node-signpdf (MIT), better-sqlite3 (MIT), and others.

telemetryDebug.title: Collected usage data (this session)
telemetryDebug.bufferedSummary_one: {{count}} event buffered (max {{max}}). Nothing has left your computer — these counts live only in memory.
telemetryDebug.bufferedSummary_other: {{count}} events buffered (max {{max}}). Nothing has left your computer — these counts live only in memory.

redaction.applyTitle: Apply redactions — this cannot be undone
redaction.applyIntro_one: You are about to redact {{rects}} area on {{pages}} page.
redaction.applyIntro_other: You are about to redact {{rects}} areas on {{pages}} page(s).
redaction.applyBulletRemoved: The marked content will be permanently removed from the PDF.
redaction.applyBulletRaster: Redacted pages become rasterized images. Non-redacted text on those pages will not be searchable until you re-run OCR (Tools → Run OCR).
redaction.applyBulletSanitize: Document metadata, embedded files, JavaScript, and annotations on redacted pages will be removed.
redaction.applyBulletIrreversible: This action cannot be undone, and is NOT part of the editor's undo stack.
redaction.applyFooter: The original file on disk is unchanged until you Save. You may Cancel and the marks remain pending.
redaction.applySaveAsHint: For safety, consider using File → Save As before applying redactions so the original file remains untouched.
redaction.applySignatureBody: Applying redaction will invalidate every signature listed. The signature audit panel will be updated to reflect this.
redaction.errorPageOutOfRange: Some marks reference removed pages — refresh and try again.
redaction.errorRectInvalid: Invalid redaction shape detected; please remark.
redaction.errorPdfLoadFailed: PDF could not be parsed for redaction.
redaction.errorRasterizeFailed: Page rasterization failed — see Help for diagnostics.
redaction.errorOutputTooLarge: Redacted output exceeded the size ceiling; reduce pages or DPI and retry.
redaction.warningSearchabilityLost: Redacted pages are now rasterized images. Re-run OCR to restore text search.
redaction.warningFormsRemoved: Form fields were removed during redaction. Flatten the form first to keep filled values.
redaction.warningStructureRemoved: Accessibility structure was removed during redaction.
redaction.warningSignaturesInvalidated_one: {{count}} digital signature was invalidated by this redaction.
redaction.warningSignaturesInvalidated_other: {{count}} digital signatures were invalidated by this redaction.

findATool.title: Find a tool
findATool.instructions: ↑↓ to navigate · Enter to run · Esc to close
